prompt.content:
Use file “Onc_Patient 7” to access the entire medical history / record for the patient. Please use the patient tab as the source of truth for all demographic information. Disregard patient identifiers (Name, Age, DOB, MRN) found within the text of the clinicalnotes tab as they have been shifted for de-identification purposes

-----

The patient and her adult daughter presented to your vascular clinic on November 6, 2022, to establish care as new patients. You reviewed her medical record to date and confirmed her medical history with her. An excerpt of your conversation with her is attached in the file "Onc_Patient 7 - AI Script.pdf". Vital signs reveal tachycardia at 114 BPM, a respiratory rate of 16, and BP of 150/84 mmHg. Her temperature is 99.8 degrees Fahrenheit. Physical exam reveals erythema and tenderness around an abdominal wound, with all other findings identical to the most recent documentation in the clinical record. Following the appointment, you sent her for bloodwork, and the results are attached in the file "Onc Patient 7 - CBC with diff.pdf". Ankle-brachial indices (ABIs) and pressures are identical to those at the last visit. 

Based on this information, stage the patient’s lower-extremity wounds using the Wound, Ischemia, foot infection (WIfI) classification and propose the optimal management plan for the lower-extremity and abdominal wounds. For this case, assume she was born in 1935.

extra.task_name: AI Scribe/Labs - Onc Patient 7 - Wound Care - Jehshua K

golden_responses.golden_text_response:
1. Based on this information, stage the patient’s lower-extremity wounds using the Wound, Ischemia, foot infection (WIfI) classification and propose the optimal management plan for the lower-extremity and abdominal wounds. For this case, assume she was born in 1935.

- The patient’s lab results show leukocytosis. The abdominal wound exhibits clinical signs suggestive of localized cellulitis. Given the patient’s history of methotrexate and steroid use, as well as his advanced age, he is considered immunocompromised and will require admission for treatment of the cellulitis associated with the abdominal wound.
- According to the last wound care note, the right proximal posterior leg wound is completely healed. The right malleolus wound is classified as WIfI stage 1. Similarly, the right distal anterior leg wound is WIfI stage 1, and the right distal posterior leg wound is also WIfI stage 1. No vascular intervention is necessary for the patient’s WIfI stage 1 wounds; wound care should continue as previously prescribed.
- There is a discrepancy between ABI and toe pressure in the left limb; the WIfI system prioritizes toe pressure for grading. The left distal midline leg wound is WIfI stage 3. The patient has a clinical symptom of coldness in the left lower extremity. Therefore, this patient should undergo evaluation for left limb revascularization.
